item.title: 使用 Appwrite 和 Twilio 进行电话身份验证 | Phone Authentication with Appwrite and Twilio
item.description:
是的，你猜对了！ 我们将在 Appwrite 中引入电话身份验证！ 在此版本中，您现在可以使用电话号码对用户进行身份验证。 此版本还引入了 3 个新的电话提供商，包括Twilio 、 TextMagic和TeleSign ！

在本教程中，我们将学习如何设置Twilio帐户并将其配置为与 Appwrite 一起使用，并构建一个 Svelte 应用程序以将它们组合在一起！ 该应用程序将演示Appwrite 0.15 中引入的所有新手机身份验证端点的用法！ 以下是我们将学习的内容：

  • 使用电话号码创建会话并进行验证。
  • 要更新用户的电话号码，请发送验证码并更新验证状态。

事不宜迟，让我们开始吧！

第一步是创建一个 Twilio 帐户。 前往注册页面，填写您的详细信息，验证您的电子邮件和电话号码。 在 Twilio 仪表板上，单击获取 Twilio 电话号码按钮。

这会生成一个电话号码，我们可以使用它来发送消息。 在 Account Info 部分下，您将找到Account SID 、 Auth Token和新生成的电话号码。 将它们放在手边，因为您将在下一步中需要它们。

在进入下一步之前，我们需要在仪表板中做最后一件事。 由于我们使用的是 Twilio 的免费试用版，因此我们只能向经过验证的电话号码发送短信。 前往Verified Callers IDs页面并输入您想向其发送消息的号码。

接下来，我们需要启动并运行 Appwrite 服务器。 您可以选择在系统本地安装它，也可以使用我们的一键设置之一将其部署在云上。

只需要一个魔法命令！ 此命令安装 Appwrite 并使用docker-compose.yml和.env文件创建一个appwrite文件夹。 安装完成后，转到appwrite文件夹。

我们需要更新.env文件中的两个变量，即_APP_PHONE_PROVIDER和_APP_PHONE_FROM 。 您可以从 Twilio Dashboard 的 Account Info 部分获取Account SID 、 Auth Token和电话号码。

完成后，我们需要重新启动 Appwrite 服务器以使环境变量生效。 请注意，此命令需要从安装期间创建的appwrite文件夹中执行。

 docker compose up -d --force-recreate

接下来，转到localhost （或您的公共 IP 地址）并创建一个新用户帐户，然后创建一个具有您选择的名称的项目。

从设置页面复制projectId和端点，因为我们将在下一步构建的 Web 应用程序中需要它！

为了这个示例，我们将使用SvelteKit ，但概念和代码示例对于所有框架仍然适用。 首先，我们需要创建一个新的 SvelteKit 项目并设置Tailwind CSS 。 我发现官方Tailwind 安装指南在这里非常有用。

我建议您完成设置并返回本教程，而不是在这里重新发明轮子。

好吧！ 是时候安装我们的一些依赖项了！ 我们将为我们的项目使用两个依赖项。 前往项目文件夹并使用以下命令安装它们

npm i -D @zerodevx/svelte-toast npm i appwrite

接下来，将以下条目添加到您的 .env 文件中。 如果此文件不存在，您可能需要创建它。

 VITE_APPWRITE_ENDPOINT = http://localhost/v1 VITE_APPWRITE_PROJECT_ID = <YOUR_PROJECT_ID]

不要忘记将值替换为您自己的 Appwrite projectId和您之前从 Dashboard 获得的端点。

接下来，让我们创建我们的 Appwrite 服务，它将帮助将所有 SDK 调用抽象到一个地方。 新建文件src/lib/appwrite.ts ，填写如下内容

import { Client , Account } from ' appwrite ' ; const client = new Client (); client . setEndpoint ( import . meta . env . VITE_APPWRITE_ENDPOINT ) . setProject ( import . meta . env . VITE_APPWRITE_PROJECT_ID ); const account = new Account ( client ); export class AppwriteService { public static async getAccount (): Promise < any > { return account . get (); } public static async createAccount ( email : string , password : string , name ?: string ): Promise < any > { return account . create ( ' unique() ' , email , password , name ); } public static async createEmailSession ( email : string , password : string ): Promise < any > { return account . createEmailSession ( email , password ); } public static async deleteSession (): Promise < any > { return account . deleteSession ( ' current ' ); } /** Phone Authentication endpoints */ public static async createPhoneSession ( number : string ): Promise < any > { return account . createPhoneSession ( ' unique() ' , number ); } public static async updatePhoneSession ( userId : string , secret : string ): Promise < any > { return account . updatePhoneSession ( userId , secret ); } public static async updatePhone ( number : string , password : string ): Promise < any > { return account . updatePhone ( number , password ); } public static async createPhoneVerification (): Promise < any > { return account . createPhoneVerification (); } public static async updatePhoneVerification ( userId : string , secret : string ): Promise < any > { return account . updatePhoneVerification ( userId , secret ); } }

在这里，我们导入 appwrite SDK 并初始化Client和Account服务。 我们还导出了一个AppwriteService类，其中包含许多可以在我们的应用程序中使用的静态方法。 所有其他文件都处理项目的 UI，并且应该相当容易阅读和理解。

为了保持本教程的简洁，我们将复制完成的GitHub 项目中所有其他文件的内容。 这是我们需要复制的文件列表

完成后，我们就可以预览项目了。 运行以下命令以运行本地开发服务器。

这将我们带到本教程的结尾！ 我希望本指南可以帮助您在 Web 应用程序中设置电话身份验证，最重要的是，我希望您喜欢学习新知识！ 我在学习 Svelte 并为本教程构建 Web 应用程序时获得了很多乐趣，我迫不及待地想构建更多应用程序！

如果您在教程中的任何时候遇到困难，请随时在我们由人类运营的友好不和谐频道中寻求帮助！ 如果您喜欢我们在 Appwrite 所做的事情，请不要忘记在 GitHub 上给我们一颗小星星 😉 这里有一些很好的资源可以帮助您入门。



原文:

Appwrite 0.15 was just announced and brings with it one of the most anticipated Appwrite features! Yes, you guessed that right! We’re introducing phone authentication in Appwrite! With this release you’ll now be able to authenticate users using their phone numbers. This release also introduces 3 new phone providers including Twilio, TextMagic & TeleSign!

In this tutorial we’ll learn how to set up a Twilio account and configure it for use with Appwrite and build a Svelte app to put it all together! The app will demonstrate the usage of all the new phone authentication endpoints introduced in Appwrite 0.15! Here's what we will learn:

  • To create sessions using a phone number and verify them.
  • To update a user’s phone number, send verification codes and update the verification status.

Without further ado, let’s get started!

🤔 New to Appwrite?

Appwrite is an open-source back-end-as-a-service that abstracts all the complexity of building a modern application by providing you with a set of REST and Realtime APIs for your core back-end needs. Appwrite takes the heavy lifting for developers and handles user authentication and authorization, databases, file storage, cloud functions, webhooks, and much more!

🧑‍💻 Setting up Twilio

The first step is to create a Twilio account. Head over to the Sign Up page, fill in your details, verify your email and phone number. On the Twilio Dashboard, click on the Get a Twilio phone number button.

This generates a phone number that we can use to send messages from. Under the Account Info section, you’ll find the Account SID, Auth Token and the newly generated phone number. Keep these handy as you’ll need them in the next step.

There’s one last thing that we need to do in the Dashboard before we move to the next step. Since we’re using Twilio’s free trial, we can only send an SMS to verified phone numbers. Head over to the Verified Callers IDs page and enter the numbers that you’d like to send messages to.

⚙️ Install Appwrite

Next, we need to get an Appwrite server up and running. You can either choose to install it locally on your system or deploy it on the cloud with one of our one click setups.

TLDR; It only takes a single magic command! 😊

docker run -it --rm \ --volume /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock \ --volume "$(pwd)"/appwrite:/usr/src/code/appwrite:rw \ --entrypoint="install" \
    appwrite/appwrite:0.15.1

This command installs Appwrite and creates an appwrite folder with docker-compose.yml and .env files. Once the installation is complete, head over to the appwrite folder.

We need to update two variables in the .env file namely _APP_PHONE_PROVIDER and _APP_PHONE_FROM. _APP_PHONE_FROM is the phone number you just obtained from Twilio and _APP_PHONE_PROVIDER is a DSN string that helps Appwrite connect to the Twilio API.

_APP_PHONE_PROVIDER takes the following format

_APP_PHONE_PROVIDER=phone://<Account SID>:<Auth Token>@twilio

_APP_PHONE_FROM takes the following format

_APP_PHONE_FROM=+14344251928

You can obtain the Account SID, Auth Token and the phone number from the Account Info section of the Twilio Dashboard.

Once that is done, we need to restart the Appwrite server for the environment variables to take effect. Please note that this command needs to be executed from the appwrite folder that was created during installation.

docker compose up -d --force-recreate

Next, head over to localhost ( or your public IP address ) and create a new user account followed by a project with a name of your choosing.

Copy the projectId and the endpoint from the Settings page as we’ll need it for the web app we build in the next step!

🔮 Setting up Our SvelteKit Project

For the sake of this example, we’ll be using SvelteKit but the concepts and code examples remain true for all frameworks out there. First, we’ll need to create a new SvelteKit project and set up Tailwind CSS. I’ve found the official Tailwind installation guide to be extremely helpful here.

Instead of re-inventing the wheel here, I’d recommend you to complete the setup and return back to this tutorial.

Alright! It’s time to install some of our dependencies! We’ll be using two dependencies for our project. Head over to the project folder and install them with the following commands

npm i -D @zerodevx/svelte-toast
npm i appwrite

Next, add the following entries into your .env file. You might need to create this file if it does not exist already.

VITE_APPWRITE_ENDPOINT=http://localhost/v1
VITE_APPWRITE_PROJECT_ID=<YOUR_PROJECT_ID]

Don’t forget to replace the values with your own Appwrite projectId and endpoint that you obtained from the Dashboard earlier.

🔨 Setting up the Appwrite SDK

Next, let’s create our Appwrite service that will help abstract all the SDK calls to a single place. Create a new file src/lib/appwrite.ts and fill in the following contents

import { Client, Account } from 'appwrite'; const client = new Client(); client .setEndpoint(import.meta.env.VITE_APPWRITE_ENDPOINT) .setProject(import.meta.env.VITE_APPWRITE_PROJECT_ID); const account = new Account(client); export class AppwriteService { public static async getAccount(): Promise<any> { return account.get(); } public static async createAccount(email: string, password: string, name?: string): Promise<any> { return account.create('unique()', email, password, name); } public static async createEmailSession(email: string, password: string): Promise<any> { return account.createEmailSession(email, password); } public static async deleteSession(): Promise<any> { return account.deleteSession('current'); } /** Phone Authentication endpoints */ public static async createPhoneSession(number: string): Promise<any> { return account.createPhoneSession('unique()', number); } public static async updatePhoneSession(userId: string, secret: string): Promise<any> { return account.updatePhoneSession(userId, secret); } public static async updatePhone(number: string, password: string): Promise<any> { return account.updatePhone(number, password); } public static async createPhoneVerification(): Promise<any> { return account.createPhoneVerification(); } public static async updatePhoneVerification(userId: string, secret: string): Promise<any> { return account.updatePhoneVerification(userId, secret); }
}

Here, we import the appwrite SDK and initialize the Client and Account services. We also export an AppwriteService class with a lot of static methods that we can use across our app. The appwrite.ts file is all it takes to integrate Appwrite into the project. All the other files deal with the UI of the project and should be fairly straightforward to read and understand.

In order to keep this tutorial concise, we’ll just copy over the contents for all the other files from the completed GitHub project. Here are a list of files we need to copy

Once complete, we’re all ready to preview the project. Run the following command to run a local development server.

That brings us to the end of this tutorial! I hope this guide helped you set up phone authentication in your web app and above all, I hope you enjoyed learning something new! I had a lot of fun learning Svelte and building a web app for this tutorial and I can’t wait to build some more apps!

📚 Resources

If you’re stuck at any point during the tutorial, feel free to reach out for help in our friendly discord channels run by humans! If you like what we do at Appwrite, don’t forget to drop us a tiny star on GitHub 😉 Here are some great resources to help you get started.
item.author: christyjacob4

item.title: 什么鬼? ? | What the hell is ?
item.description:
<!DOCTYPE html> <!-- What the hell is this?? -->> <html lang="en"> <head> <title>Document</title> </head> <body> </body> </html

在创建新的 HTML 文件时，您可能想知道，“为什么我需要将此语句添加到我的 HTML 文件的第一行？ 虽然这是真的，但还有更多。 在我们理解上面的陈述之前，让我们试着理解一下DTD是什么。

文档类型定义 (DTD)

文档类型定义（或简称 DTD）描述了 XML 文档的文档结构以及文档可以具有的合法元素的列表。

在 HTML5 之前（例如在 HTML 4.01 中），所有 HTML 文档都必须在其第一行包含“Doctype”声明以及对其类型定义的引用。 这看起来像：

 <!DOCTYPE HTML PUBLIC "-//W3C//DTD HTML 4.01//EN" "http://www.w3.org/TR/html4/strict.dtd">

该声明有两个目的：

  • 提供文档可以包含的有效元素列表。
  • 定义 DTD 的风格，可以是严格的、过渡的或框架集。 更多关于这个here 。

那么，为什么在您遇到的最新 HTML 文件中没有看到这些长的 DTD 引用呢？

输入 HTML5！

请看，HTML 的早期版本（HTML5 之前）是基于 SGML（标准通用标记语言）的，因此需要引用 DTD。 从 HTML5 开始，不再需要此参考，因为此版本的 HTML 不是基于 SGML。

所以现在，这个“Doctype”声明服务于另一个目的。 它允许浏览器以“标准模式”运行 HTML 文件。 现在顺理成章地问，“现在这个标准模式到底是什么？？” 要理解这一点，让我们回到过去。

时光倒流

当技术爱好者意识到所谓的互联网的力量时，两种流行的浏览器应运而生——Netscape Navigator 和继其成功之后，微软的 Internet Explorer。 这甚至在 W3C 标准出现之前，该标准最终将标准化代码在不同浏览器中的运行方式。 因此，您可以想象，鉴于这些浏览器的流行，网站被编写成两个版本——一个用于 Navigator，另一个用于 IE，现在听起来可能有点多余，但在当时是常态。 然而，在创建 W3C 标准并且浏览器开始遵循这些标准之后，开发人员遇到了新的问题。

现在的问题是遗留代码开始出现问题。 因此，一个可能的解决方案是网站现在有两个版本 - 一个标准版本（我们之前提到的那个），它符合 W3C 标准，因此可以在不同的浏览器上运行，另一个支持遗留代码的Quirks 版本.

现在浏览器如何识别它需要使用哪种模式？ 好吧，只需在 HTML 文件的第一行添加一个有效的 DOCTYPE 声明，以指示浏览器以标准模式运行代码。 除此之外的任何东西都会在 IE9 或更早版本中触发Quirks 模式。 这正是<!DOCTYPE html>从 HTML5 开始所做的。 如果您未能将此行添加到 HTML 文件中，浏览器会将其解释为在Quirks 模式下运行代码的指令，您最终可能会在不同浏览器中得到不一致的结果。

结论

考虑到这个小背景，我希望您现在了解 <!DOCTYPE html> 语句的重要性。 如果您想阅读更多有关此内容的信息，请浏览我在下面提供的链接作为参考。

参考

https://www.freecodecamp.org/news/what-is-the-doctype-declaration-in-html/ https://www.freecodecamp.org/news/what-is-the-doctype-declaration-in-html/
https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Quirks_Mode_and_Standards_Mode https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Quirks_Mode_and_Standards_Mode
https://hsivonen.fi/doctype/ https://hsivonen.fi/doctype/



原文: 
<!DOCTYPE html> <!-- What the hell is this?? -->>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <title>Document</title>
</head>
<body>
</body>
</html

When creating a new HTML file, you might have wondered, “Why do I need to add this statement to the first line of my HTML file? Doesn’t the .html file extension suffice in telling the browser that this is an HTML file?”. While this is true, there’s still more to it. Before we understand the above statement, let’s try to understand what a DTD is.

Document Type Definition (DTD)

A Document Type Definition (or DTD in short) describes the document structure of an XML document along with a list of the legal elements the document can have.

Prior to HTML5 (for e.g. in HTML 4.01), all HTML documents would have to contain a “Doctype” declaration on its first line along with a reference to its type definition. This would look something like:

<!DOCTYPE HTML PUBLIC "-//W3C//DTD HTML 4.01//EN" 
"http://www.w3.org/TR/html4/strict.dtd">

This declaration served two purposes:

  • Provide the list of valid elements the document can contain.
  • Define the flavour of the DTD which could be strict, transitional or frameset. More on this here.

So why don’t you see these long DTD references in the latest HTML files you've encountered?

Enter HTML5!

See, earlier versions of HTML (prior to HTML5) were SGML (Standard Generalized Markup Language) based, and therefore required a reference to DTDs. HTML5 onwards, this reference was no longer needed since this version of HTML is not SGML based.

So now, this “Doctype” declaration serves another purpose. It allows browser to run the HTML file in a “Standard mode”. Now it’s logical to ask, “What on earth is this standard mode now??” To understand this, let’s go back in time.

Back in time

Back when tech enthusiasts realised the power of what would be known as the internet, two popular browsers came into existence - the Netscape Navigator & following its success, (drumrolls) Microsoft’s Internet explorer. This was even before the W3C standards came into picture which would eventually standardise how code would run across different browsers. Hence, you can imagine that, given the popularity of these browsers, websites were written in two versions - one for the Navigator, and the other one for IE, which might sound a little redundant now, but was the norm back then. However, after the W3C standards were created and browsers started adhering to them, developers encountered a new problem.

The problem now was that the legacy code started to break. Hence, a possible solution to this was that the sites were now made in two versions - a Standard version (the one which we mentioned earlier) which was W3C standards compliant and hence would run across different browsers and a Quirks version which supported the legacy code.

Now how do browsers identify which mode it needs to use? Well, just add a valid DOCTYPE declaration in the first line of the HTML file, to instruct the browser to run the code in Standard mode. Anything other than that will trigger the Quirks mode in IE9 or older. This is exactly what <!DOCTYPE html> does HTML5 onwards. If you fail to add this line to your HTML file, the browser would interpret this as an instruction to run your code in Quirks mode, and you could end up getting inconsistent results across different browsers.

Conclusion

With this little background in mind, I hope you now understand the importance of the <!DOCTYPE html> statement. If you’d like to read a little more on this, please go through the links I have provided as a reference below.

References

https://www.freecodecamp.org/news/what-is-the-doctype-declaration-in-html/
https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Quirks_Mode_and_Standards_Mode
https://hsivonen.fi/doctype/
item.author: aman894

item.title: 使用 Appwrite 0.15 提升您的 Webhook 安全性 | Level up Your Webhook Security With Appwrite 0.15
item.description:
我们将大部分繁琐、重复的任务从您手中抽离出来，例如身份验证、数据库、存储等。 因此，您可以专注于构建项目，而不是重新发明轮子。

为什么要验证 Webhook？

只需一个人即可找到您的 webhook URL，将无效和不安全的数据发送到您的后端； 这可能会导致有人告诉您的后端有人创建了会话（例如，当他们没有创建会话时），并且可以让他们访问其他用户的帐户。 他们可以做的另一件事是说数据库条目已被编辑，这可能会在后端毒化您的本地副本，从而允许欺诈数据。 如果您不验证 webhook，这只是冰山一角。

那么如何验证 Webhook？

验证 webhook 再简单不过了！ 您需要通过用于在标头中生成签名的相同算法运行 webhook 有效负载，然后将两者进行比较。 如果它们匹配，则确认 webhook 来自您的 Appwrite 实例。

为了开始启用验证，您需要获取 webhook 的签名密钥，这可以在 webhook 属性页面的仪表板中找到。 对于那些想知道签名密钥是什么的人，它是用于散列有效负载并生成“x-appwrite-webhook-signature”标头的密钥。 请注意，您绝不应该共享或释放此密钥，因为它允许第三方伪造有效负载签名。 您应该将其存储在后端的安全位置，无论是在 .env 文件中还是在机密管理器中。 如果此密钥泄露，您可以随时在 webhook 设置中更改密钥。

要生成要比较的哈希，您需要使用签名密钥通过 HMAC SHA1 函数运行附加到 URL 的有效负载，然后将结果转换为 Base64。 之后，您将其与“x-appwrite-webhook-signature”标头进行比较。 根据后端编写的语言，实现这一点会有所不同。 例如，nodeJS 看起来像这样： 

 const crypto = require ( ' crypto ' ); let token = crypto . createHmac ( " sha1 " , process . env . WEBHOOK_SIG_KEY ) . update ( `https://yourwebhookurl/test ${ payload . body } ` ) // Make sure there isn't a space between the URL and body. . digest (). toString ( ' base64 ' ); if ( token !== payload . headers [ ' x-appwrite-webhook-signature ' ]) { throw new Error ( ' Failed authentication check. ' ) }

在 PHP 中，它可能看起来像这样：

 <?php $token = base64_encode ( hash_hmac ( 'sha1' , 'https://yourwebhookurl/test' . $payload . body , getenv ( 'WEBHOOK_SIG_KEY' ), true )); if ( $token != $payload . headers [ 'x-appwrite-webhook-signature' ]) { throw new Error ( 'Failed authentication check.' ); }

请记住，此代码可能与您在后端编写的不同。

查看官方发布说明，并继续关注更多博文！ 在接下来的几周里，我们将在他们单独的博文中描述所有功能，以给他们当之无愧的聚光灯。 我们迫不及待地想看看您使用 Appwrite 构建了什么！ 让·卡洛斯·莫洛西 - 一旦暂停，appwrite 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，appwrite 将能够再次评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，appwrite 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 appwrite 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。



原文:

Appwrite is an Open-Source BaaS, which sounds complex but is actually simple. We abstract most of the tedious, repetitive tasks away from you, such as Authentication, Databases, Storage and more. So instead of reinventing the wheel, you can concentrate on building your project.

Why Should I Validate Webhooks?

Webhooks send a lot of sensitive data to your backend. It takes just one person to find your webhook URL to send invalid and insecure data to your backend; this could result in someone telling your backend that someone created a session for example (when they didn’t) and could give them access to other user’s accounts. Another thing they could do is say that database entries were edited, which could poison your local copy on the backend allowing for fraudilent data. This is just the tip of the iceberg of what could happen if you don’t validate your webhooks.

How Do I Validate Webhooks, Then?

Validating webhooks couldn't be easier! You need to run the webhook payload through the same algorithm used to generate the signature in the header and then compare the two. If they match, the webhook is confirmed to come from your Appwrite instance.

In order to begin enabling validation your going to need to grab your webhook’s signature key, this can be found in the dashboard in your webhook’s properties page. For those of you wondering what a signature key is, it’s the key that is used to hash the payload and generate the ‘x-appwrite-webhook-signature’ header. Please note that you should NEVER share or release this key as it allows a third party to forge the payload signature. You should store this in a secure place on your backend, either in a .env file or a secrets manager. If this key ever gets leaked you can always change the key within your webhook’s settings.

To generate the hash to compare, you'll need to run the payload appended to the URL through an HMAC SHA1 Function using the signature key, then convert the result into Base64. After that you compare it to the ‘x-appwrite-webhook-signature’ header. Implementing this will be different depending on the language your backend is written. For example, nodeJS will look like so:

const crypto = require('crypto');
let token = crypto.createHmac("sha1", process.env.WEBHOOK_SIG_KEY) .update(`https://yourwebhookurl/test${payload.body}`) // Make sure there isn't a space between the URL and body. .digest().toString('base64'); if (token !== payload.headers['x-appwrite-webhook-signature']) { throw new Error('Failed authentication check.')
}

and in PHP, it may look like this:

<?php $token = base64_encode(hash_hmac('sha1', 'https://yourwebhookurl/test' . $payload.body, getenv('WEBHOOK_SIG_KEY'), true)); if ($token != $payload.headers['x-appwrite-webhook-signature']) { throw new Error('Failed authentication check.');
}

Please remember that this code may differ from what you write in your backend.

📚 Learn More

Webhook validation is only the tip of the iceberg of what Appwrite 0.15 offers! Check out the official release notes, and stay tuned for more blog posts! In upcoming weeks we will describe all features in their separate blogposts to give them well-deserved spotlights. We can't wait to see what you build with Appwrite! 😎

Jean Carlos Molossi - Jun 29

Once suspended, appwrite will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, appwrite will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by appwrite will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If appwrite is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.
item.author: ionicisere

item.title: VS Code 中所有分支的早期合并冲突检测 | Early Merge Conflict Detection Across All Branches in VS Code
item.description:
我们很高兴地宣布 GitLive 的旗舰功能，早期合并冲突检测，现在适用于 VS Code 的所有分支！ 当我们第一次引入实时合并冲突检测时，只有每个队友当前分支的更改会显示在 gutter 中。 这意味着您可能会因当前未处理的分支中的更改而错过合并冲突，例如，来自等待审查的 PR 的更改。 现在，编辑器底部的更改指示器显示所有包含与当前分支相比的更改的分支的更改。

而且，和以前一样，只要您不处于离线模式，这包括您的队友未处于离线模式的未推送更改，用于实时、即时的合并冲突检测！

新的排水沟指示器过滤器

以前，您有一个简单的开关来显示或隐藏您在编辑器的装订线中看到的指标。 现在，与团队窗口类似，您可以按类型过滤编辑器的装订线中显示的更改指示器。

值得注意的是，这些过滤器独立于团队窗口过滤器，但是当您更改团队窗口过滤器时，装订线指示器过滤器会自动更新以匹配（反之亦然）。 要打开装订线过滤器菜单，请单击 VS Code 右上角的 GitLive 图标。

关闭推送的更改将隐藏来自其他分支上推送的更改的指标。 关闭未推送更改将隐藏来自队友未推送更改的指标。 默认情况下，关闭非冲突更改以仅在装订线中显示冲突。

默认情况下，隐藏 3 个月内未修改的分支的更改，打开此选项以显示它们。

现在就是这样，我们很想知道您对这些更新的看法以及我们如何使 GitLive 更好地为您服务，因此请通过下面的评论部分联系我们。

还没有安装 GitLive 扩展？ 您可以在此处找到它，如果想了解有关最新版本的更多信息，请查看此博客文章。

一旦暂停，gitlive 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，gitlive 将能够再次评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，gitlive 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 gitlive 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。



原文:

We are happy to announce that GitLive's flagship feature, early merge conflict detection, now works across all branches in VS Code! 🎉

When we first introduced real-time merge conflict detection, only the changes from each of your teammates’ current branch were shown in the gutter. This meant you could miss a merge conflict from changes in a branch that is not currently being worked on, for instance, changes from a PR waiting for review. Now the change indicators in the gutter of the editor show changes from all branches that contain changes compared to your current branch.

And, as before, and as long as you are not in offline mode, this includes the unpushed changes of your teammates that are also not in offline mode for real-time, instant merge conflict detection!

New gutter indicator filters

Previously you had a simple switch to show or hide the indicators you see in the gutter of the editor. Now, similar to the team window, you can filter the change indicators shown in the gutter of your editor by type.

It’s worth noting that these filters are independent of the team window filters, but when you change the team window filters the gutter indicator filters are automatically updated to match (but not vice versa). To open the gutter filters menu, click on the GitLive icon in the upper right corner of VS Code.

🔸 Pushed Changes. Turning off pushed changes will hide the indicators coming from the pushed changes on other branches. 🔸 Unpushed Changes. Turning off unpushed changes will hide the indicators coming from the unpushed changes of your teammates. 🔸 Non-conflicting changes. On by default, turn off non-conflicting changes to only show conflicts in the gutter.

🔸 Include stale changes. By default, changes from branches that have not been modified in 3 months are hidden, turn this on to show them.

👉 Both pushed and unpushed changes are on by default, turn both off to hide the indicators completely

That’s it for now, we would love to know what you think of these updates and how we can make GitLive better for you, so please reach out via the comments section below.

Don't have the GitLive extension installed yet? You can find it here, and if want to learn more about the latest release, check out this blog post.

Sharad Raj (He/Him) - Jun 13

Yehezkiel Gunawan - Jun 22

Once suspended, gitlive will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, gitlive will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by gitlive will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If gitlive is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.
item.author: stecagnieszka

item.title: 立即开始暂存代码的 5 个理由 | 5 reasons to start staging your code right now
item.description:
在软件开发中，登台是在将代码投入生产之前在实时环境中测试代码的过程。 登台环境几乎是生产环境的复制品，因此您可以准确地看到发布代码后用户将体验到什么。 开发团队通常在三种类型的环境中使用多个实例：

  • 本地环境——每个开发人员都有一个。 在这里，代码致力于开发流的各个分支并在本地进行预览。

  • 暂存环境- 在实时环境中运行代码以进行测试。 开发人员可能会在即将推出时或在开发阶段更早的时候发布候选版本以验证 git 推送。 阶段可以直接连接到主分支，也可以连接到尚未合并到主分支的功能分支。

  • 生产环境——运行项目的实时版本。

为什么开发团队经常跳过阶段

作为生产环境的精确副本，登台环境很容易需要与维护生产环境相同的努力。 如果多个候选版本同时处于暂存状态，那么额外的工作量可能会成倍增长。

更重要的是，staging 为开发人员的日常工作又增加了一步。 除非绝对必要，否则他们并不完全热衷于这一点。 如果我的项目在本地主机上看起来不错，为什么要上台，对吧？

但我们不应该太仓促。 分期有很多好处。 并且手头有合适的工具，分期完全不需要额外的成本或努力。

分期的好处

你的设计师更快乐

与设计师一起在前端项目上工作？ 让他们有机会检查最新版本的外观是否符合他们的期望。 从经验来看，不愉快的意外可能会削弱团队精神。

客户知道会发生什么

概念创建过程中的误解很常见。 即使部署完全按计划进行，您的客户也可能有不同的想法。 在上线之前向他们发送预览链接以调整期望。

不再尴尬“在我的机器上看起来不一样”的时刻

谁没去过？ 一个实时项目突然出现问题，但负责人声称他们在本地计算机上没有这个问题。 如果您在暂存阶段严格测试多个场景，这些场景是完全可以避免的。

责任是共担的

当然，您可以要求队友审查您的拉取请求。 这样一来，您就不是唯一在发布前看到代码的人。 但是 PR 并不能取代成熟的阶段。 通过阶段，您可以将基本质量检查委托给 QA 或测试工程师。

每个人都在循环中

没有水晶球可以告诉项目经理你在本地机器上的代码有多远。 使用暂存环境让他们不时看到进度会让他们高兴——并且可能使您免于回复焦虑的电子邮件。

改变分期游戏

在本地创建一个功能分支，将其推送到 staging，实时查看您的代码，合并到生产环境并自动关闭 staging 环境。

这就是使用Deploy Now进行分期的容易程度。

每个 Deploy Now 包都提供暂存分支作为受 SSL 保护的预览 URL。 与客户和团队成员共享这些 URL，或使用它们在不同的设备和浏览器上检查您的代码。

无需额外的成本和精力来管理额外的基础设施，您还应该跳过这个阶段吗？ 绝对不。

如果您已经与多个分支机构合作，您的日常工作流程甚至不会改变。 您的代码是自动构建和部署的，因此在实时环境中尽早并经常运行您的代码非常快速和简单。

那你呢？ 为什么你认为每个开发团队都应该使用暂存环境？

一旦暂停，ionos 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，ionos 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦未发布，ionos 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 ionos 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。



原文:

In software development, staging is the process of testing your code in a live environment before pushing it to production. A staging environment is pretty much a replica of a production environment, so you can see exactly what users will experience once you release the code. Development teams often work with multiple instances within three types of environments:

  • Local environment – every developer has one. Here, code is committed to various branches of development streams and previewed locally.

  • Staging environment – runs code in a live environment for testing purposes. Developers might stage release candidates as they approach rollout, or much earlier in the development phase to verify git pushes. Stages can be connected directly to the main branch, or to feature branches which are not yet merged into master.

  • Production environment – runs the live version of a project.

Why development teams often skip the stage

As an exact replica of your production environment, a staging environment can easily require the same effort that goes into maintaining the production environment. And that additional effort can grow exponentially if multiple release candidates are all in staging at the same time.

What’s more, staging adds yet another step to a developer’s daily. Which is something they’re not exactly keen on unless it’s absolutely necessary. Why stage if my project looks fine on localhost, right?

But we shouldn’t be too hasty. Staging has a lot of benefits. And with the right tools at hand, staging requires no extra cost or effort at all.

The benefits of staging

Your designers are happier

Working with a designer on a project with frontend? Give them a chance to check whether the look and feel of the latest release meets their expectations. Speaking from experience, unpleasant surprises can put a dampener on the team spirit.

Clients know what’s coming

Misunderstandings during concept creation are common. Even if a deployment goes exactly as planned, your client might have had something different in mind. Send them a preview link to align expectations before going live.

No more awkward “it looked different on my machine” moments

Who hasn’t been there? An issue flares up with a live project, but the person responsible claims they didn’t have this issue on their local machine. These scenarios are completely avoidable if you rigorously test for multiple scenarios during the staging phase.

Responsibility is shared

Sure, you can ask teammates to review your pull request. That way you weren't the only person who saw the code before release. But PRs don’t replace fully-fledged stages. With stages you can delegate essential quality checks to QA or test engineers.

Everyone stays in the loop

There's no crystal ball showing project managers how far you are with the code on your local machine. Using staging environments to let them see the progress from time to time will make them happy – and probably save you from replying to anxious emails.

Changing the staging game

Deploy Now is a build tool created by developers for developers for building and hosting static site generators, PHP apps and single page applications on custom engineered IONOS infrastructure.
Create a feature branch locally, push it to staging, view your code live, merge to production and automatically shut down the staging environment.

That's how easy staging with Deploy Now is.

Every Deploy Now package offers staging branches as SSL-secured preview URLs. Share these URLs with clients and team members or use them to check your code on different devices and browsers.

With no extra cost and effort required for managing additional infrastructure, should you still skip the stage? Absolutely not.

If you work with multiple branches already, your daily workflow won't even change. Your code is built and deployed automatically, so it’s super quick and simple to run your code in a live environment early and often.

What about you? Why do you think every dev team should use staging environments?

Once suspended, ionos will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, ionos will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by ionos will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If ionos is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.
item.author: roberts

item.title: 作为程序员赚取额外收入的 11 个愚蠢的简单副业 | 11 Stupidly Simple Side Hustles to Earn Extra Money as a Programmer
item.description:
在过去的几年里，我涉足了各个领域，掌握了一些新技能，开展了一些副业，并结识了一些才华横溢的人。

由于这一切，我学到了更多。 我们现在有一个志同道合的人网络，他们互相帮助赚钱或增加我们的钱。

举个例子：我的一个朋友一直在尝试投资股票，当他发现一些有趣的东西时，他会与我们分享，以便我们可以投入几周或几个月的钱。

一位是技术企业家，他讨论了最新的技术趋势以及我们如何投资这些趋势。 由于他的建议，我们将部分资金投入到去中心化土地上，并了解了更多关于 Web3 的知识。

我们相信与他人分享可以让我们学到更多，发现最好的机会，并获得更多。

现在，我想与您分享一些您可能会研究、了解并从中赚钱的副业。 此外，不要期望任何不正当的工作或不需要工作的副业。 因为这种类型的副业不存在。

毫无疑问，每一个侧面的喧嚣都很容易，因为我们大多数人（我，以及我的朋友）都做过并且正在赚钱。 然而，每一面的喧嚣都需要一致性和一些努力。

注意：不会有附属链接，网站在一定程度上都可以免费使用。 换句话说，我不会从他们的主人那里获得任何奖励。

让我们开始吧。

是的，你没看错。 一些网站可能会通过销售代码来帮助您赚钱。

但具体如何？ 一些艺术家、企业家和公司所有者需要网站来进行他们的工作。 为此，他们必须聘请自由职业者，使用 WordPress 或其他网站编辑器，或购买网站。

好吧，必须有人出售才能购买网站。 卖家是程序员，更具体地说是开发人员。

例如，Git Market 和 Codecanyon 是您可以出售代码的两个地方。 这是一个提供网站和应用程序代码的网站。 有数以千计的 PHP 脚本、移动应用程序模板、HTML5 模板、JavaScript 代码和其他资源。

有几个网站和应用程序代码可供选择。 例如，如果您想创建一个电子商务商店，您可以支付 Codecanyon 并获得您的电子商务网站或应用程序。

还有什么好说的？ 您不必支付 1000 美元。 有几个网站出售价格从 5 美元到 50 美元不等的代码。

同样，您可以开发网站、应用程序或插件，并在 CodeCanyon 或 GitMarket 上销售它们。

老实说，我还没有出售任何 Notion 模板。 是的，我在 Gumroad 和 Etsy 上销售过 Canva 模板、数字产品，以及在 Codecanyon 上的代码。

我的第一个想法是是否有人通过 Notion 收到了钱。 而答案比你预想的还要出乎意料。

这是现实生活中的一个例子。

因此，其中一位读者通知我，他在前三个月使用 Notion 赚了大约 1,500 美元。

那不是很棒吗？ 每天工作 2-3 小时后。

其次，我在 Twitter 上发现一个名叫Easlo的人声称他本月从 Notion 赚了 1000 美元。

因此，如果您想开始使用或了解更多信息，您可以观看一些 YouTube 教程。

销售 API 是要考虑的最新和最受欢迎的副业之一。 此外，如果您是 Web 开发人员，则不必学习新技术，因为您主要使用 API。

有一些平台，例如 RapidAPI，开发人员可以在其中销售 API 并赚钱。

之后，我在上面观看了一个 YouTube 视频。 然后我输了做别的东西。
后来，我收到一家公司的电子邮件，要求我开发 API 供他们销售。 我真的被吓了一跳。 他们是赚钱，还是只是向我的收件箱发送垃圾邮件？ 所以我试图通过他们的联系安排一次会议并与他们交谈。

在讨论了我的技能并了解之后，我问他们是否从他们的业务中赚钱。

而且，为了完成这项工作，我们需要您每天工作 3-4 小时。

因此，如果您了解 JavaScript 或 Python，则可以开始从事这项副业。

如果我告诉你创意经济的钱比世界其他地方的总和还多呢？

您是否知道程序员享有许多额外的好处？

以下是这背后的两种解释：

  • 您可能知道 LinkedIn 引入了创建者模式，并且 Medium 已收购 Knowable 以向网站添加音频。

  • 程序员的需求量很大，所以如果你知道如何编码，你就可以教书、帮助、提供价值并赚钱。

你想做什么完全取决于你。 您可以创建 YouTube 课程和课程以在 Skillshare 和 Udemy 等网站上销售。

如果您不想制作视频，您可以在 Medium 上发布而不是制作视频。 您还可以创建有关新资助、购买或推出的技术或企业的内容。

根据我的经验，你会赚很多钱，而且你的收入会随着你提供更多的价值而增长。

最好的入门方法是什么？ 从你现在知道的开始； 你不需要成为专家。

让我告诉你一个发人深省的事实。 首先，你不会在第一年赚到任何钱。 一个原因是你还处于学习阶段，必须专注于学习新方法和创造价值。

从第二年开始，你会赚很多钱，真的很多。 但是，请记住，权力越大，责任越大。 这完全取决于你如何接近和实现。

我的一些同事使用 Figma 或 Adob​​e XD 创建网站。 他们中的大多数人并不知道我们可能会使用 Canva 模板并对其进行自定义以满足我们自己的需求。

后来我的其他朋友也试过了，他们的工作量明显减少了。 因为他们可以很容易地在 Canva 上进行设计，所以他们可以通过向客户销售来赚取更多的钱。

让我告诉你我与一家网站设计公司一起参加的另一场活动。 我和一位每天只工作三个小时的平面设计师一起工作。 当我问他时，他说他订阅了 Canva 专业版，每次他想制作一些东西时，他都会去 Canva。

下一个是在 Canva 上销售模板。

那么，你知道谁在 Canva 中制作模板吗？ 像我们这样的设计师。
有一个名为Canva Creators的页面，您可以在其中申请，如果被接受，您可以通过在 Canva 上销售模板来赚钱。 他们目前只与选定的创作者合作。 如果你知道如何设计，你可以申请。

我还遇到了一些通过向客户教授 Canvas 来赚钱的人。

如果您创建 Canva 设计并在 Canva 或 Gumroad 上出售它们，您会发现自己很容易赚钱。

大多数工程师在顶级 IT 企业甚至初创公司工作，因此他们精通加密货币和股票等技术。

结果，绝大多数开发人员将能够创建与加密、NFT 和其他热门技术相关的任何东西。

您可能还知道 NFT、Defi 和 Virtual Land 正在引起很多关注。 更不用说新的元界发明了。

为此，您还可以掌握区块链技术。 此外，提供绝大多数人需要的服务。

因此，如果您是一名开发人员，那么您在学习新技术并从中赚钱方面具有优势。

毫无疑问，您必须深入研究这些技术。

您可能听说过 Amazon KDP、Redbubble、Gumroad、Etsy 和其他市场，人们在这些市场上出售他们的物品，以便在每次有人购买它们时赚钱。

我的朋友约翰建议我写一本 80 到 100 页的书，然后提交给 Amazon KDP。 我测试了它，你会惊讶地听到我每个月都会收到一份 Kindle 佣金。

我的好友乔治在 Twitter 上拥有大量粉丝，他还在 Gumroad 上免费提供了他的第一本书。 然而，大部分客户为此付出了代价。
在三个月内，他筹集了 656 美元。

此外，我的朋友 Alia 全职工作并尝试制作 T 恤和靠垫。 所以她在 Redbubble、Teespring 和 Zazzle 上卖掉了一些设计，五个月内赚了大约 2341 美元。

同样，如果你有东西要做，你可以自由地做，并以各种方式出售。

如果您具有专业知识，您可能会为企业或公司提供建议而赚钱。

让我从我自己的经历中举一个例子。 六个月前，LinkedIn 的一家公司找到我，说他们正在寻找一位有网络编程经验的顾问。

我要求他们提供更多信息。 所以他们告诉我，他们需要一个开发人员，可以在他们遇到重大困难时帮助他们的开发人员，并在客户会议上回答客户的一些问题。

这里的所有都是它的。 我不需要从 9 点到 5 点编写代码或工作。我同意，因为我熟悉 Next.js 和 React.js，所以这对我来说很简单。

我的其他朋友，例如山姆，按小时收取咨询费。 此外，一些公司聘请他为他们的目的更新最好的技术堆栈。

不是很酷吗？ 我知道，作为一个开始，你将无法更深入地了解这些副业。 但是，在中级水平，您可以尝试这种形式的副业。

当我看到诸如“为什么一只带有流行馅饼身体的动画飞猫以近 600,000 美元的价格售出”之类的头条新闻时，我有点紧张。 我认为 NFT 的概念是一个诡计，它依赖于运气。

也许你对这些也有同样的想法。

然而，我们大多数人都错了。

如果实施得当，它有可能扰乱许多部门。 除了数字艺术品，NFT 还可以以多种方式应用。

例如，确保物品的真实性。 假冒食品，如补充剂和药物，是当前全球关注的问题； 想象一下，扫描您在线购买的营养补充剂上的二维码，并查看补充剂从制造到交付的完整路径。

甚至音乐家也可以使用智能合约技术将其版权转让给 NFT，确保每次播放歌曲时都能获得报酬。

还有更多的例子。

为什么这么简单？ 因为其他人没有很好地利用 NFT。 因此，如果你是一名程序员，你可以为各种目的建立一个 NFT 市场。

我是一名 Web 开发人员，对支持 Web3、区块链和元界的技术感兴趣，这在我们的社会中引起了如此大的轰动。

更有趣的是，对于像我这样的 web 开发者来说，成为 web3 开发者是很简单的。

我不需要学习很多新技术； 我只需要知道如何将 Solidity、Truffle 和 Ganache 与 Web 技术一起使用。

根据众多民意调查，绝大多数人不了解 Defi、Web3 或 CBDC。 此外，不要认为区块链仅对银行业或数字支付有益。 当然，它的意义远不止于此。

它可能对投票流程、供应链管理、法规遵从性、审计、物联网操作系统和许多其他应用程序很有用。
请允许我向您透露一些秘密。

绝大多数 IT 巨头已经开始创建区块链相关技术。 此外，大多数组织已经开始为 web3 工程师支付更多费用。

总之，如果你是一名 Web 开发者，你可以快速了解区块链技术并利用它来赚取更多的钱。

在我的一位朋友向我提到他之后，我最近与卢卡斯聊天。 他是一名专业开发人员，主要为一家法国大公司工作。

并且，由于他的理解，他尝试开发电子书并在线销售。 一开始，他认为这是一项副业。

出版第十本书后不久，他的收入猛增。 他通常每月由亚马逊支付一次。 他还告诉我，他的书现在可以在 Kindle 上找到。 结果，他将能够赚到更多的钱。

我催促他了解更多细节。 您能否详细说明您遇到的挑战？ 所以他告诉我，当我第一次开始写书时，我尝试了 Python 简介和 React 入门等名称。

但是，附近有很多书。 结果，我无法工作。 更具体地说，从亚马逊上此类出版物的数量可以看出，任何人都可以创作此类书籍。

所以你想知道我现在做了什么吗？ 简而言之，我努力利用我所拥有的知识来写短篇小说。 它顺利进行。

我认识到，我们大多数人将无法从我列出的所有副业中赚钱。 尝试从一个开始，然后再到其他。

而且，正如我在某些情况下所展示的那样，您必须相信收入是可以实现的。

准确地说，这篇文章是我过去写过的许多故事的混搭。

如果您想查看更多此类内容，可以在Patreon上支持我！

祝你今天过得愉快！



原文:

Over the previous several years, I’ve dabbled in a variety of sectors, picked up a few new skills, launched a handful of side hustles, and met some brilliant individuals.

And as a result of all of this, I’ve learned a lot more. We now have a network of like-minded people who help each other make or increase our money.

Let me give you an example: one of my friends is constantly trying to invest in stocks, and when he discovers something intriguing, he shares it with us so that we may put money into it for a few weeks or months.

One is a tech entrepreneur who discussed the most recent technological trends and how we might invest in them. We invested part of our money in decentralized land as a result of his advice and learned a lot more about Web3.

We believe that sharing with others allows us to learn a lot more, uncover the finest opportunities, and earn a lot more.

And now, I’d want to share with you a few side hustles that you may look into, learn about, and make money from. Also, don’t anticipate any shady employment or a side hustle that requires no work. Because this type of side hustle doesn’t exist.

Without a doubt, each and every side hustle is easy because the majority of us(I, as well as my friends) have done it and are making money. However, every side hustle necessitates consistency and some effort.

Note: There will be no affiliate links, and the websites will all be free to use up to a point. In other words, I will not earn any reward from their owners.

Let’s get started.

1. Earn Money from Selling Code

Yes, you read that correctly. Some websites might assist you in earning money by selling codes.

But how exactly? Some artists, entrepreneurs, and company owners require websites for their work. And for that, they must engage a freelancer, use WordPress or another website editor, or purchase websites.

Well, someone has to sell in order to purchase a website. And the seller is a programmer, more specifically a developer.

Git Market and Codecanyon, for example, are two places where you can sell your code. It is a website that provides website and app code. There are thousands of PHP scripts, mobile app templates, HTML5 templates, JavaScript code, and other resources.

There are several websites and app codes to choose from. For example, if you want to create an e-commerce store, you may pay Codecanyon and get your e-commerce website or app.

What else is there to say? You do not have to pay $1000. There are several websites that sell codes ranging in price from $5 to $50.

Similarly, you may develop websites, applications, or plugins and sell them on CodeCanyon or GitMarket.

2. Selling Templates on Notion

To be honest, I haven’t sold any Notion templates yet. Yes, I’ve sold Canva templates, digital items on Gumroad and Etsy, and code on Codecanyon.

The first thought that came to me was whether anyone had received money via Notion. And the answer was even more unexpected than you had anticipated.

Here’s an example from real life.

So, one of the readers notified me that he had made roughly $1,500 in the preceding three months using Notion.

Isn’t that great? Every day, after 2–3 hours of work.

Secondly, I found a guy on Twitter named Easlo claiming that he earned $1,000 this month from Notion.

So, if you want to get started or learn more about it, you may watch some YouTube tutorials.

3. Selling APIs

Selling APIs is one of the newest and most popular side hustles to consider. What’s more, if you’re a web developer, you don’t have to learn new technologies because you’re mostly working with APIs.

There are platforms, such as RapidAPI, where developers may sell API and make money.

After that, I viewed a YouTube video on it. Then I lost to make something else.
Later, I got an email from a company asking me to develop APIs for them to sell. I was really taken aback. Do they make money, or are they just spamming my inbox? So I attempted to arrange a meeting through their connection and spoke with them.

I asked if they were generating money from their business after discussing my skills and learning about it.

“We can’t share our whole revenues with you, but let me tell you that we are receiving close to 15–20 clients every month,” they claimed. And, in order to accomplish the job, we need you to work 3–4 hours every day.

So, if you know JavaScript or Python, you can get started with this side business.

4. Become an Online Creator

What if I told you that the creative economy has more money than the rest of the world combined?

And did you know that programmers enjoy a plethora of additional benefits?

The following are the two explanations behind this:

  • Top firms like LinkedIn, Facebook, and even Medium are investing in hiring more creators. You may be aware that LinkedIn has introduced a creator mode, and that Medium has acquired Knowable in order to add audio to the site.

  • Programmers are in high demand, so if you know how to code, you can teach, help, provide value, and earn money.

And what you want to do is absolutely up to you. You may create YouTube lessons and courses to sell on sites like Skillshare and Udemy.

You can publish on Medium instead of making videos if you don’t want to do them. You may also create content about newly funded, purchased, or launched technology or enterprises.

According to my experience, you will make a lot of money, and your earnings will grow as you provide more value.

What is the best way to get started? Begin with what you know now; you don’t need to be an expert.

Let me tell you a sobering truth. For starters, you will not make any money in your first year. One reason for this is that you are still in the learning stage and must focus on learning new methods and creating value.

Beginning in the second year, you will earn a lot, literally a lot. However, keep in mind that with great power comes great responsibility. And it is absolutely up to you how you approach and achieve.

5. Earning with Canva

Some of my colleagues use Figma or Adobe XD to create websites. And the bulk of them are unaware that we may use Canva templates and customize them to meet our own demands.

Later, other of my friends tried it, and their workload decreased noticeably. Because they can easily make designs on Canva, they may earn more money by selling to their clients.

Let me tell you about another event I attended with a website design firm. I worked with a graphic designer who only worked three hours every day. When I questioned him, he said he has a Canva pro subscription and that every time he wanted to produce something, he went to Canva.

The next one is by selling templates on Canva.

So, do you know who makes the templates in Canva? Designers like us.
There is a page called Canva Creators where you may apply and, if accepted, make money by selling templates on Canva. They are presently only working with select creators. You may apply if you know how to design.

The Canva team will send you an email at some time.

I’ve also met with some people that make money by teaching Canvas to their clients.

If you create Canva designs and sell them on Canva or Gumroad, you’ll see that you’re earning money easily.

6. Crypto, Defi, NFT, and Virtual Lands

Most engineers work at top IT businesses and even startups, so they are well-versed in technologies such as cryptocurrency and stock.

As a result, the vast majority of developers will be able to create anything related to Crypto, NFTs, and other hot technologies.

You may also be aware that NFTs, Defi, and Virtual Land are generating a lot of attention. Not to mention the new Metaverse invention.

For this aim, you can also master Blockchain technology. Also, provide a service that the vast majority of people require.

As a result, if you are a developer, you have an edge when it comes to learning about new technology and making money from it.

Without a doubt, you must delve more into these technologies.

7. Selling the Products

You’ve probably heard of Amazon KDP, Redbubble, Gumroad, Etsy, and other marketplaces where people sell their items in order to gain money every time someone buys them.

My friend John suggested that I write an 80–100-page book and then submit it to Amazon KDP. And I tested it, and you’ll be astonished to hear that I get a Kindle commission every month.

My buddy George, who has a sizable Twitter following, had also made his first book available for free on Gumroad. The bulk of clients, though, paid for it.
Within three months, he collected $656.

In addition, my friend Alia works full-time and tries to create t-shirts and cushions. So she sold some of the designs on Redbubble, Teespring, and Zazzle, earning roughly $2341 in five months.

Similarly, if you have something to make, you may do it freely and sell it in a variety of ways.

8. Become an Online Consultant

If you have specialized knowledge, you may generate money advising a business or firm.

Let me give you an example from my own experience. Six months ago, one of the LinkedIn businesses approached me and said they were seeking a consultant with some Web programming experience.

I asked for more information from them. So they informed me that they need a developer that can help their developers when they run into a major difficulty and be there at the client conference to answer some of the customer’s questions.

That’s all there is to it. I’m not required to code or work from 9 to 5. And I agreed because I’m familiar with Next.js and React.js, so it was simple for me.

Others of my friends, such as Sam, charge an hourly amount for advice. Furthermore, some firms hire him to update the best technology stack for their purposes.

Isn’t it cool? I understand that as a beginning, you won’t be able to go deeper into these side hustles. However, at an intermediate level, you can experiment with this form of side hustle.

9. Make an NFT Marketplace, Industry, or Application

When I see headlines like Why an Animated Flying Cat With a Pop-Tart Body Sold for Almost $600,000, I become a little nervous. I assumed that the notion of NFTs was a ruse and that it was reliant on luck.

Maybe you’re thinking the same thing about these.

However, the majority of us are mistaken.

The notion of NFTs is far more than we ever imagined; if implemented correctly, it has the potential to disrupt a number of sectors. Beyond digital artworks, NFT may be applied in a variety of ways.

Assuring the authenticity of items, for example. Fake food goods, such as supplements and medication, are a current concern around the globe; NFTs can assist tackle this by tracking and tracing food products. Imagine scanning a QR code on a nutritional supplement you purchased online and viewing the supplement’s full path, from manufacture to delivery.

Even musicians may employ smart contract technology to transfer their copyrights to NFTs, ensuring that they are paid every time their song is played.

And there are also plenty of more examples.

Why is it so simple? Because others aren’t making good use of NFTs. As a result, if you’re a programmer, you may build an NFT marketplace for a variety of purposes.

10. Becoming a Web3 Developer

I’m a web developer who is interested in the technology that underpins Web3, Blockchain, and the metaverse, which is generating such a stir in our society.

What’s more intriguing is that becoming a web3 developer is straightforward for a web developer like myself.

I don’t need to learn a lot of new technology; all I need to know is how to use Solidity, Truffle, and Ganache with web technologies.

According to numerous polls, the vast majority of people are uninformed of Defi, Web3, or CBDC. Also, don’t think about blockchain as solely being beneficial in the banking sector or for digital payments. There’s a lot more to it than that, of course.

It may be useful for voting processes, supply chain management, regulatory compliance, auditing, IoT operating systems, and many other applications.
And please allow me to divulge a few secrets to you.

The vast majority of IT behemoths have begun to create blockchain-related technology. Furthermore, most organizations have started to pay more for web3 engineers.

In a word, if you’re a web developer, you can learn about blockchain technology rapidly and utilize it to generate more money.

11. Write Technical Content-related Books

I recently chatted with Lucas after one of my friends mentioned him to me. He is a professional developer who works largely for a major French firm.

And, as a result of his understanding, he attempts to develop ebooks and sell them online. In the beginning, he thought of it as a side job.

His earnings skyrocketed shortly after releasing his tenth book. He is normally paid once a month by Amazon. He also informed me that his book was now available on Kindle. He will be able to earn more money as a consequence.

I prodded him for further details. Could you perhaps elaborate on the challenges you’ve encountered? So he informed me that when I first started writing books, I attempted names like Introduction to Python and Getting Started with React.

However, there are numerous books nearby. As a result, I am unable to work. More specifically, as seen by the number of such publications on Amazon, anybody may create such books.

So you want to know what I’ve done now? Simply put, I endeavored to write short books using the knowledge I possessed. It went off without a hitch.

I recognize that most of us will not be able to produce money from all of the side hustles I listed. Try starting with one and then on to others.

And, as I’ve shown in certain instances, you must believe that earning is achievable.

To be precise, this post is a mash-up of numerous stories I’ve written in the past.

*That’s it and if you found this article helpful, please hit the ❤️, 🦄 button and share the article, so that others can find it easily :) *

If you want to see more of this content you can support me on Patreon!

Have a nice day!
item.author: kai_wenzel

item.title: 我们如何在大型项目中压缩数据 | How we compress data in large projects
item.description:
塔兰图尔

发表于大家好！ 我叫 Alexander Klenov，在 Tarantool 工作。 在本文中，我想详细讨论其中一项功能——RAM 中的数据压缩。 我会告诉你如何使用它，它可以做什么和不能做什么，以及需要注意哪些方面。

如何启用数据压缩

这很容易做到——只需在字段的设置中指定它需要被压缩：compression = ' < option > '。

有两种可用的压缩选项：

例如：

 local space = box.schema.space.create(space_name, { if_not_exists = true }) space:format({ {name = 'uid', type = 'string'}, {name = 'body', type = 'string', compression = 'zstd'}, })

如果您的项目已经有一些上传的数据，则需要执行后台迁移：

 box.schema.func.create('noop', { is_deterministic = true, body = 'function(t) return t end' }) space:upgrade{func = 'noop', format = { {name = 'uid', type = 'string'}, {name = 'body', type = 'string', compression = 'zstd'}, }}

有关更多详细信息， 请参阅文档。

使用数据压缩可以实现什么？

所以，我们已经设置了这个功能。 让我们看看它能给我们带来什么。 例如，我们将使用一家大型电信公司的真实数据。 数据包括 10 万个不同的 JSON 文档，总容量为 316 MB。

我们将尝试不同的压缩机制。 在我们的案例中，CPU 容量为 3.6 GHz。 为了更好地看到差异，让我们使用外部 ZLIB 库添加压缩。

测试显示以下结果：

请参阅GitHub 上的测试源代码。

关键结论是所有这三种方法的工作方式不同。 使用 ZLIB 进行压缩与 ZSTD 的结果相当，并且不会太慢，但解压速度相当低。

集群中的压缩

在实际项目中，Tarantool 实例是按角色划分的。 对于我们的示例，区分两个角色很重要：

压缩集群数据不像只压缩一个节点那样是一项线性任务。 有几种方法可以做到这一点，具体取决于执行压缩的位置。 这些方式各有优缺点。 我们来看三个例子。

选项一——快速的结果。

在这里，压缩和解压缩发生在存储中。 这可以通过本文中描述的功能来实现。 当您需要以最少的更改快速释放大量空间时，它很有用。

听起来不错，但这会给存储的 CPU 带来额外的负载并减慢它的速度。 此外，每个副本都会重复这种压缩。 结果，整个集群的性能下降。 带有内置打包器的存储扩展成本更高。 您需要安装一个带有打包器的存储，而不是一个额外的打包器。

在此处查看如何执行此操作的示例：

路由器中的局部变量 enable_zlib 必须设置为 false。 在 storage 的第 11 行中，您必须指定压缩类型。

当您需要快速释放空间时，此选项非常有用。 它作为一种临时解决方案效果很好，但如果负载允许，它也可以成为永久解决方案。 它既便宜又快速。

但是，如果您想将集群性能保持在同一水平，则需要付出更多的努力。

选项二——最高性能

压缩和解压缩发生在路由器中。 这种方法提供了更好的性能，但内置的压缩​​在这里不起作用——你必须连接一个外部库并为其实现额外的逻辑。 主服务器和副本服务器将接收已经打包的数据。

作为示例，我们将使用上述路由器-存储对。 除了这里，必须将路由器中的 enable_zlib 变量设置为 true。 在这种情况下，我们不需要在存储中指定压缩类型。

优点：

缺点：

如果我们需要更大的灵活性，我们可以创建一个单独的角色来管理数据压缩。 它将允许将路由器的数量与压缩实例的数量分开配置。 资源利用将变得更加灵活。 我们来看看第三个选项。

选项三——惰性压缩

我们在单独的实例中压缩数据并在路由器中解压缩。 因此，我们将数据按原样写入存储，未压缩。 然后我们实现一个单独的打包者角色，它通过主存储并打包所有未打包的内容。 如果新数据上传速度过快，您需要更快地打包，您可以简单地添加更多打包器实例。 副本将接收已打包的数据。

为了更好地理解这个概念，请参见此处的示例。

可以用相同的负载测试进行测试。 该测试将显示与未压缩写入相同的结果，因为压缩不会在写入期间发生，而是在单独的并行线程中发生。

优点：

缺点：

如果出现以下情况，它会很有用：

将压缩移动到路由器

在查看了不同的选项之后，让我们看看当我们将压缩从存储移动到路由器时会发生什么，因为这是最有效的方法。 让我们构建一个包含两个实例的小型集群——一个路由器和一个存储。 然后，我们将在使用不同的压缩方法时加载它。

测试台实现：

用于生成数据的补充模块。

最大测试持续时间设置为 30 秒。 等待测试完成的时间不会太长，另一方面，测试负载的时间也足够了。 在此期间，我们代表 10 个虚拟用户向路由器生成 100,000 个写入请求。 请参阅此处的控制台输出。 结果总结在下表中。

基于关键参数——每秒请求数 (RPS)、延迟 (http_req_duration) 和总请求执行时间 (iteration_duration)——路由器上的压缩比存储上的压缩性能要好得多。

这主要是因为 ZSTD 压缩比 ZLIB 压缩需要更长的时间。 此外，从路由器到存储的数据传输已经压缩，因此写入存储的数据更少。 另一个优点是您不需要通过压缩加载存储。

结论

压缩数据的最佳方式取决于您的项目的具体情况——因此是使用建议。

如果您不需要或没有能力使用外部库，您可以使用内置的 LZ4 压缩工具。 这种方法允许释放大约 40% 的空间，几乎没有 RPS 下降（6351 使用 LZ4 与 7076 没有压缩）。 只需单击一下即可启用它，在我看来，这是一个很大的优势。

如果您想要更好、更高效或更便宜的数据压缩，则需要付出额外的努力，如上所述。

您可以在官方网站上下载 Tarantool并在我们的Telegram 聊天中获得帮助。

一旦暂停，tarantool 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，tarantool 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，tarantool 发布的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 tarantool 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。



原文:

tarantool

Posted on Jun 29

Hi everyone! My name is Alexander Klenov, and I work in Tarantool. In April, we released Tarantool 2.10 Enterprise Edition–an updated version of the Tarantool in-memory computation platform. The 2.10 version includes several new features.

In this article, I want to talk in detail about one of the features–data compression in RAM. I am going to tell you how to use it, what it can and cannot do, and what aspects to pay attention to.

How to enable data compression

It's very easy to do–just specify in the field's settings that it needs to be compressed: compression = ' < option > '.

There are two compression options available:

• zstd,
• lz4.

For example:

local space = box.schema.space.create(space_name, { if_not_exists = true })
space:format({
    {name = 'uid', type = 'string'},
    {name = 'body', type = 'string', compression = 'zstd'},
})

If your project already has some uploaded data, you'll need to perform a background migration:

box.schema.func.create('noop', {
    is_deterministic = true, body = 'function(t) return t end'
})

space:upgrade{func = 'noop', format = {
    {name = 'uid', type = 'string'},
    {name = 'body', type = 'string', compression = 'zstd'},
}}

See the documentation for more details.

What can be achieved by using data compression?

So, we've set up the feature. Let's see what it can give us. As an example, we'll use real data of a major telecom company. The data includes 100 thousand different JSON documents with a total volume of 316 MB.

We will try out different compression mechanisms. The CPU capacity in our case is 3.6 GHz. To see the difference better, let's add compression using the external ZLIB library.

Testing showed the following results:

*ZLIB external library

See the source code of the test on GitHub.

The key conclusion is that all these three methods work differently. ZSTD compresses efficiently but slowly, although it unpacks documents rather quickly. LZ4 compresses and unpacks quickly, but the compression ratio is lower than in other methods. Compression using ZLIB is comparable to the results of ZSTD and is not too slow, but the decompression speed is pretty low.

Compression in a cluster

In real-live projects, Tarantool instances are divided by roles. For our example, it's important to distinguish two roles:

• Router
• Storage

Compressing cluster data is not a linear task like compressing just one node. There are a couple of ways to do it, depending on where the compression is performed. These ways have their advantages and disadvantages. Let's take a look at three examples.

Option one — quick result.

Here, compression and decompression happens in the storage. This can be implemented with the functionality described in this article. It's useful when you need to free a lot of space quickly with minimal changes.

Sounds good, but this creates additional load to the storage's CPU and slows it down. Moreover, each replica will be repeating this compression. As a result, the performance of the entire cluster decreases. A storage with a built-in packer is more expensive to scale. Instead of one additional packer, you'll need to install a storage with a packer in it.

See an example of how to do this here:

The local variable enable_zlib in the router must be set to false. In the storage, in the 11th line, you must specify the compression type.

This option is good when you need to free space quickly. It works well as a temporary solution, but can also become a permanent one, if the load allows it. It's cheap and fast.

But if you want to keep the cluster performance on the same level, it would take more effort.

Option two — maximum performance

Compression and decompression happens in the router. This method offers better performance, but the built-in compression won't work here — you have to connect an external library and implement additional logic for it. The master and replicas will receive already packed data.

As an example, we'll use the router — storage pair described above. Except here, the enable_zlib variable in the router must be set to true. We don't need to specify the compression type in the storage in this case.

Advantages:

• We won't load the storage. • We are reducing the traffic between the router and the storage.

• It's easier to scale — we just need to set up the necessary number of routers with a packer until we reach the required level of performance.

Disadvantages:

• We need to implement packer logic on the router.
• A delay on the router due to compression and decompression appears.

If we need more flexibility, we can create a separate role to manage data compression. It would allow configuring the number of routers separately from the number of compression instances. Resources utilization will become more flexible. Let's take a look at the third option.

Option three — lazy compression

We compress the data in a separate instance and decompress it in the router. So, we write the data to the storage as it is, uncompressed. Then we implement a separate packer role that goes through master storages and packs everything that is not packed. If new data is uploaded too fast, and you need to pack more quickly, you can simply add more packer instances. The replicas will receive already packed data.

For better understanding of this concept, see an example here.

It can be tested with the same load test. The test will show the same results as writing without compression, since the compression does not occur during writing but in a separate parallel thread.

Advantages:

• There is no lag on the router for data write. • Data compression scales with maximum efficiency. • There are less cluster instances.

• We don't load the storage as in the first case.

Disadvantages:

• Additional traffic in the cluster — between the storage and the packer. • Additional load on the storage and one more read and write operation. • We need to implement a separate role — packer.

• We need to implement unpacking logic on the router.

It can be useful if:

• You want to write data quickly — at once, without compression.
• You want to regulate compression speed by increasing or decreasing the number of instances, and use resources more flexibly.

Moving compression to the router

After looking at different options, let's see what happens when we move compression from the storage to the router, since this is the most efficient method. Let's build a small cluster with two instances — a router and a storage. Then, we'll load it while using different compression methods.

Test bench implementation:

• Router implementation.
• Storage implementation.
• Module for write load testing. Supplemental module for generating data.

The maximum test duration is set at 30 seconds. It is not too long to wait for the tests to finish, and on the other hand, it's enough time to test the load. During this time, we generate 100,000 write requests to the router on behalf of 10 virtual users. See the console output here. The results are summarized in the table below.

Based on the key parameters — number of requests per second (RPS), latency (http_req_duration), and total request execution time (iteration_duration) — compression on the router performs much better than compression on the storage.

This is primarily because ZSTD compression takes longer than ZLIB compression. Also, data from the router to the storage is transferring already compressed, so less data is written to the storage. Another advantage is that you don't need to load the storage with compression.

Conclusion

Tarantool keeps growing and gains new features. The compression mechanism built into 2.10EE is useful, but it can't solve everything. The best way to compress data depends on the specifics of your project — hence the recommendations for use.

If you do not have the need or the ability to use an external library, you can use the built-in LZ4 compression tool. This method allows freeing about 40% of the space with almost no RPS drawdown (6351 with LZ4 vs. 7076 without compression). And it can be enabled in just one click, which, in my opinion, is a great advantage.

If you want better, more efficient, or cheaper data compression, you'll need to make additional efforts, which are described above.

You can download Tarantool on the official website and get help in our Telegram chat.

Vignesh Muthukumaran - Jun 15

Once suspended, tarantool will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, tarantool will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by tarantool will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If tarantool is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.
item.author: tarantool

item.title: 带条件的 Visual Studio 断点 | Visual Studio breakpoint with conditions
item.description:
费利佩·马克斯

发表于这是一个简单而快速的技巧，也许你已经知道了，但我不知道，即使在每天使用 Visual Studio 和 C# 工作了 10 年后，所以我决定写下它。

如果您曾经必须调试需要多次尝试评估特定条件下的行为的代码，那么您已经需要类似条件断点之类的东西。 正如它的名字所说，VS 为我们提供了这个功能。

为了举例说明，我编写了一个简单的代码，在控制台上打印 10 个随机数。

 Console.WriteLine("Begin"); var rnd = new Random(); for (int i = 1; i <= 10; i++) { Console.WriteLine($"{i}: {rnd.Next(10)}"); } Console.WriteLine("End"); Console.ReadLine();

如果您设置断点，如下图所示，调试将在循环的所有 10 次执行中停止。

但是，您必须在条件为i==5时观察循环行为，在这种情况下，右键单击断点并放置您的条件。

条件是在语言中指定的，您可以使用该范围内可用的任何变量，在这种情况下，我使用的是i变量。

这是整个过程的简单示例视频。

希望您喜欢，如果您不知道此功能，请在此处发表评论。

丹尼尔·戈麦斯·贾拉米洛—— 米卡林德利 - 一旦暂停，felipemsfg 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，felipemsfg 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，felipemsfg 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，并且只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 felipemsfg 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。



原文:

Felipe Marques

Posted on Jun 29

🇧🇷 Versão em português

This is a simple and quick tip, maybe you already know it, but I don't, even after 10 years working daily with visual studio and C#, so I decided write about it.

If you've ever had to debug code that takes several attempts to evaluate behavior on just a certain condition, you already needed something like a breakpoint with conditions. And as its name says, VS provides this feature for us.

To exemplify, I made a simple code that prints 10 random numbers on a console.

Console.WriteLine("Begin");
var rnd = new Random();

for (int i = 1; i <= 10; i++)
{
    Console.WriteLine($"{i}: {rnd.Next(10)}");
}

Console.WriteLine("End");
Console.ReadLine();

If you put a breakpoint, as in the image below, the debug will stop at it all 10 executions of the loop.

However, you can must observe the loop behavior just when the condition is i==5, in this case, right click on the breakpoint and put your condition.

The conditions are specified as in the language and you can use any variable available in that scope, in this case I'm using the i variable.

Here is a simple example video of the whole process.

I hope you like and if you don't know this feature, comment there.

Daniel Gomez Jaramillo - Oct 25 '19

Micah Lindley - Sep 10 '19

Once suspended, felipemsfg will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, felipemsfg will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by felipemsfg will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If felipemsfg is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

item.title: 将我的 Pytorch 模型转换为 Pytorch Lightning | Convert my Pytorch model to Pytorch Lightning
item.description:
包彼得

发表于大家好！ 今天我将向您展示如何将我的模型从Pytorch转换为Pytorch Lightning 。 它删除了许多样板代码（几乎可以在任何深度学习管道中找到的标准代码），并添加了许多有助于在特定位置干扰训练的功能。

首先，我导入库。

 pip install pytorch-lightning import pytorch_lightning as pl

前向函数可以在 pl 类中定义。

 # an nn class can be converted to a pl class by replacing nn with pl class NeuralNet(nn.Module): # --> class NeuralNet(pl.LightningModule): def __init__(self, input_size, num_classes): super(NeuralNet, self).__init__() self.fc1 = nn.Linear(input_size, 50) self.fc2 = nn.Linear(50, num_classes) # --> specific functions belong to nn class should not be changed! def forward(self, x): out = self.fc1(x) out = torch.sigmoid(out) out = self.fc2(out) return out

在这里阅读更多。

一旦暂停，qbaocaca 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，qbaocaca 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，qbaocaca 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 qbaocaca 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。



原文:

Peter Bao

Posted on Jun 29

Hello, everybody! Today I am going to show how you how to convert my model from Pytorch to Pytorch Lightning. Pytorch Lightning is a light-weight deep learning framework built upon Pytorch. It removes a lot of boilerplate code (standard code that can be found in almost any deep learning pipeline) and adds in many functions that helps to interfere training at a specific position.

Firstly, I import the libraries.

pip install pytorch-lightning
import pytorch_lightning as pl

Pytorch LightningModule resembles nn.Module. Forward function can be defined in a pl class.

# an nn class can be converted to a pl class by replacing nn with pl

class NeuralNet(nn.Module): 
# --> class NeuralNet(pl.LightningModule):
    def __init__(self, input_size, num_classes):
        super(NeuralNet, self).__init__()
        self.fc1 = nn.Linear(input_size, 50)
        self.fc2 = nn.Linear(50, num_classes)

# --> specific functions belong to nn class should not be changed!
    def forward(self, x):
        out = self.fc1(x)
        out = torch.sigmoid(out)
        out = self.fc2(out)
        return out

Read more here.

Marshall Nyasha Chikari - May 25

Mangabo Kolawole - May 24

Once suspended, qbaocaca will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, qbaocaca will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by qbaocaca will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If qbaocaca is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

item.title: 在 Azure 门户上创建虚拟机 | Creating Virtual Machine on Azure Portal
item.description:
阿尼凯特·库马尔·辛哈

发表于虚拟机是系统上的虚拟环境。 假设您的系统上有一个 Linux 操作系统，但您想使用 Windows 来实现某些特定功能，但又不想在您的系统上安装它。 您可以做的是，只需创建一个带有 Windows 操作系统的 VM（虚拟机），并以您想要的任何方式在您的系统上虚拟使用。
所以基本上，虚拟机 (VM) 是一个虚拟环境，它作为一个虚拟计算机系统运行，具有自己的 CPU、内存、网络接口和存储，创建在物理硬件系统上。

所以，让我们在 Azure 上创建一个 VM。

移动到Azure 门户，然后点击带有文本Create a resource的 + 图标。

您将在下面看到类似的界面。 从给定选项中选择虚拟机。

会打开这个界面。

现在让我们检查给定的每个选项。

让订阅保持原样。 在Resource group中，让我们使用输入栏下方以蓝色显示的Create new选项创建一个新组。

我正在创建一个名为VM_RG的资源组。

现在问题出现了，什么是资源组？
因此，资源组是一个容器，其中包含 Azure 解决方案的相关资源。 资源组可以包括解决方案的所有资源，或者仅包括您希望作为一个组进行管理的那些资源。

是时候给我们的虚拟机起个名字了。

  • 在虚拟机名称中，我将 VM 命名为VMonPortal 。
  • 在Region下，选择离您最近的区域。 我选择该地区作为印度中部，因为它离我最近。
  • 将可用性选项保留为不需要基础架构冗余。
  • 让Security 类型保持为Standard 。
  • 在Image下选择您想要在虚拟机中使用的操作系统。 我正在选择Windows 11 Pro 。
  • 将Azure Spot 实例保留为未选中状态。
  • 在尺寸下，选择适合您口袋的尺寸。 我选择了最低的一个，即Standard_D2s_v3 。

是时候设置我们虚拟机的用户名和密码了。
您可以看到有一些与用户名和密码相关的条件，因此请相应地选择用户名和密码。

记住这个用户名和密码，因为这将帮助你登录你的虚拟机。

保持入站端口规则部分不变。

勾选许可确认。

现在您的界面应该看起来与此类似，具有相似的值。

现在点击下面的下一步：磁盘>按钮。

现在这个界面将打开，它基本上要求您在 VM 中提供所需的数据磁盘。

我将OS 磁盘类型保持为Standard SSD ，其他项目保持不变。

如果你想添加一个数据盘，但我不添加任何，其他项目保持不变。

现在点击下面的下一步：网络 >选项卡。

我将所有项目原样保留在“网络”选项卡中。

现在直接点击下面的Review + Create按钮。

现在只需点击下面的创建按钮。
这将使用您提到的配置创建 VM。

创建过程可能需要一些时间。

这种消息可能会出现在您的屏幕上，表明部署已完成。

现在点击转到资源按钮。

虚拟机的门户将打开。

惊人的！！
我们刚刚在 Azure 门户上创建了我们的虚拟机。

可是等等！！！！
现在如何使用虚拟机。

让我们看看如何启动我们的虚拟机。

你可以看到这个酒吧。

在这里，您可以选择Connect 。 因此，您需要点击连接箭头并从下拉菜单中选择RDP选项。

现在，在您的下载文件夹中，您可以看到一个带有 VM 名称的 .rdp 文件。

只需打开该文件。

远程桌面连接界面将打开，您需要点击连接按钮以连接到您的虚拟机。

但是您需要点击下面给出的更多选择选项。 并且您需要选择Use a different account 选项。

现在在新界面上，您需要添加在创建 VM 时设置的用户名和密码。 然后点击下面的确定按钮。

如果出现这种界面，只需点击下面的是。

现在您可以看到虚拟机正在打开。

您可以放大虚拟机并使其全屏显示并像您自己的系统一样使用它。

耶耶！
现在，您终于在 Azure 门户上创建了自己的虚拟机并使用了它。

享受你的新系统🎉

克里斯托斯·马茨卡斯 - 一旦暂停，aniketkumarsinha 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，aniketkumarsinha 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦未发布，aniketkumarsinha 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 aniketkumarsinha 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。



原文:

Aniket Kumar Sinha

Posted on Jun 29

Virtual machine is a virtual environment over your a system. Assume you have a Linux OS on your system, but you want to use Windows for some specific functionality, but you don't want to install it over your system. What you can do is, just create a VM (Virtual Machine) with Windows OS and use in whatever way you want virtually over your system.
So basically, a virtual machine (VM) is a virtual environment that functions as a virtual computer system with its own CPU, memory, network interface, and storage, created on a physical hardware system.

So, let's create a VM on Azure.

Move to the Azure Portal, and tap on the + icon with text Create a resource.

You will get similar interface show below. Choose the Virtual machine from the given option.

This interface will open up.

Let's now examine each option given.

Let the Subscription be as it is. And in Resource group, let's create a new one using the Create new option shown in blue below the input bar.

I am creating a Resource group named as VM_RG.

Now the question arise that, What is a Resource group?
So, a resource group is a container that holds related resources for an Azure solution. The resource group can include all the resources for the solution, or only those resources that you want to manage as a group.

The time to give our Virtual Machine a name.

  • In the Virtual machine name, I am giving the VM a name as VMonPortal.
  • Under Region, choose the region which is nearest to you. I am choose the region as Central India as it's nearest to me.
  • Keep the Availability option as No infrastructure redundance required.
  • Let the Security type remain as Standard.
  • Under Image choose the operating system which you want in your virtual machine. I am choosing Windows 11 Pro.
  • Leave the Azure Spot instance as unchecked.
  • Under Size, choose the size which suites your pocket. I am choosing the lowest one, i.e, Standard_D2s_v3.

Time to set the username and password of our VM.
You can see that there are some criteria related to the username and password, so choose the username and password accordingly.

And do remember this username and password, because this will help you to signin to your VM.

Leave the Inbound port rules section as it is.

Check mark the licensing confirmation.

Now your interface should look similar to this, with similar values.

Now tap on Next: Disks > button below.

Now this interface will open up which basically ask for the data disks you want in your VM.

I am keeping the OS disk type as Standard SSD, leaving the other items same.

If you want you add a data disk, but I am not adding any, leaving the other items as it is.

Now tap on the Next: Networking > tab below.

I am keeping all the items as it is, in the Networking tab.

Now directly tap on Review + Create button below.

The Review + create tab will open up with some information and costing of your VM.

Now just tap on the Create button below.
This will create the VM with the configurations you mentioned.

The creation process may take some time.

This kind of message can come up on your screen that the deployment is complete.

Now tap on Go to resource button.

The portal for the virtual machine will open up.

Awesome!!
We just now created our Virtual Machine on Azure Portal.

But wait!!!!
How to use the Virtual Machine now.

Let's see how we can start our Virtual Machine.

You can see this bar.

Here you have the option of Connect. So, you need to tap on the arrow with Connect and choose the RDP option from the dropdown menu.

Connect with RDP interface will open up, and now we need to download the RDP file using the Download RDP File button.

Now in your downloads folder, you can see a .rdp file with the name of your VM.

Just open that file.

Remote Desktop Connection interface will open up, and you need to tap on the Connect button to connect to your VM.

Windows Security interface will open up. But you need to tap on More choices option given below. And there you need to choose Use a different account option.

Now on the new interface, you need to add the username and password which you set while creating the VM. And then tap on OK button below.

If this kind of interface comes up, just tap Yes below.

Now you can see the Virtual Machine is opening up.

You can enlarge the VM and make it full screen and use it like your own system.

Yayy!
Now finally you have created your own Virtual Machine on Azure Portal and used it too.

Enjoy your new system 🎉

Christos Matskas - Jan 25

Once suspended, aniketkumarsinha will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, aniketkumarsinha will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by aniketkumarsinha will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If aniketkumarsinha is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.
item.author: aniketkumarsinha

item.title: 我很高兴来到这里。 | Finnaly joined the team of devs at 'dev.to'. I am happy to be here.
item.description:
伊曼纽尔·阿帕

发表于因此，在浏览 GitHub 时，我偶然发现了一个将我链接到“dev.to”的存储库，这让我之前从未听说过它感到惊讶。

我想探索并看看我是否可以学到更多，与更多新人和开发人员建立联系，从事项目工作并度过愉快的时光。

玛丽亚·安东内拉🦋 - 马特·安杰洛桑托 - 一旦暂停，legadaxx 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，legadaxx 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦未发布，legadaxx 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 legadaxx 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。



原文:

Emmanuel Appah

Posted on Jun 29

So while going through GitHub, I got chanced to come across a repo that linked me over to 'dev.to', which is surprising that I hadn't heard of it before.

I want to explore and see if I can learn more, connect to more new people and fellow devs, work on projects and have a good time.

Maria Antonella 🦋 - Jun 29

Matt Angelosanto - Jun 28

Once suspended, legadaxx will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, legadaxx will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by legadaxx will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If legadaxx is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.
item.author: legadaxx

item.title: API的tl; dr概述？ | A tl;dr overview of an API ?
item.description:
• 什么是 API？
  • 有哪些不同类型的 API？
  • 什么是 Restful API？
  • 与 API 相关的 HTTP 动词是什么？
  • 与 API 相关的 HTTP 状态代码是什么？

在这里，服务合同称为应用程序接口，具有不同功能的应用程序称为应用程序程序，这就是 API 代表APPLICATION PROGRAMMING INTERFACE的方式。

有哪些不同类型的 API？

有两种方法可以对 API 进行分类。

开放 API / 公共 API

  • 合作伙伴 API

  • 内部 API / 私有 API

  • 复合 API

    • 在当今世界，大多数 API 都是 REST 驱动或 RESTful（基于 REST 架构）。

    什么是 Restful API？

    如果遵循这六个约束，任何 API 都可以是 RESTful API。

    • 统一接口- 将客户端与服务器分开的协议。 系统中的资源（由 API 返回的信息）应该只有一个逻辑 URI，但它应该包含指向相关 URI 以获取相关信息的链接（称为 HATEOAS）。

    • 客户端-服务器- 客户端和服务器必须能够独立发展而不相互依赖。

    • 无状态- 服务器不会存储有关客户端发出的最新请求的任何信息。 它将每个请求都视为一个新请求。 在这里，客户端负责管理应用程序的状态。

    • 分层系统- 应用程序架构应该分布在多个层中，但每一层不需要了解除中间层之外的任何其他层。

    • 按需代码（可选） - 大多数情况下是可选的。 但它通过返回可执行代码来支持您的应用程序的一部分来工作。

    与 API 相关的 HTTP 动词是什么？

    有 5 个最常见的 HTTP 动词，用法如下。

    • 它几乎类似于具有不同动词的 CRUD 功能。

      与 API 相关的 HTTP 状态代码是什么？

      与成功请求相关的 2xx 系列。

      • 重定向相关的3xx系列

        • 与客户端错误相关的 4xx 系列

          • 400 - Bad request 400 - 错误请求

          • 401 - Wrong credentials ( unauthorized ) 401 - 错误的凭据（未经授权）

          • 403 - Lack of authorization ( forbidden ) 403 - 缺乏授权（禁止）

          • 404 - Resource not exist 404 - 资源不存在

          • 405 - HTTP verb mismatch 405 - HTTP 动词不匹配

          • 422 - Send data in valid 422 - 发送有效数据

          5xx series related to Server Error 5xx系列与Server Error相关

          • 500 - Internal Server error 500内部服务器错误

          • 501 - If server don't know how to handle the request 501 - 如果服务器不知道如何处理请求

          • 502 - Bad Gateway 502错误的网关

          • 503 - Service is down for maintenance 503 - 服务因维护而停止

          • 504 - Gateway timeout 504网关超时



    原文:
    • What is an API ?
    • What is the use of an API ?
    • What are the different types of API ?
    • What is a Restful API ?
    • What are the HTTP verbs related to an API ?
    • What are the HTTP status code related to an API ?

    API is more like an agreement or a contract of services between any application with distinct functions. Here, the contract of service referred as an application INTERFACE and application with distinct functions referred as an application PROGRAMS, and that’s how API stands for APPLICATION PROGRAMMING INTERFACE.

    What is the use of an API ?

    API is mostly used for interaction( by transferring data ) between more than two applications followed by a request-response behavior.

    What are the different types of API ?

    There are two ways you can categorized an API.

    1.Based on architecture, for example

    • RPC ( Remote Procedure Call ), there are two variation XML-RPC, JSON-RPC

    • SOAP ( Simple Object Access Protocol )

    • REST ( Representational State Transfer )

    • GraphQL

    2.Based on availability, for example

    • Open API / Public API

    • Partner API

    • Internal API / Private API

    • Composite API

    In today’s world most of the APIs are REST Driven or RESTful ( based on REST architecture ).

    What is a Restful API ?

    There are six constraints in a REST API or a RESTful API. Any API can be a RESTful API if it follows those six constrains.

    • Uniform Interface - An agreement that separates clients from the server. A resource( information that returned by an API ) in the system should have only one logical URI but it should contain links (known as HATEOAS) pointing to relative URIs to fetch related information.

    • Client-Server - Client and the Server must be able to evolve separately without depending upon each other.

    • Stateless- The server will not store anything about the latest request that the client made. It will treat every request as a new one. Here the client is responsible for managing the state of the application.

    • Layered System - A application architecture should be distributed in multiple layers but each layer doesn't need to know anything about any other layer other than the intermediate one.
